original_image_tags: This is a faded, low-contrast scan of an Office Memorandum. The typed text from the reverse side of the paper is bleeding through, making it highly illegible. The text is mirrored horizontally and appears in a faint blue/grey against the aged paper background. The primary visible text is the header and some fragmented body text.

elements.description: A scanned document page showing the reverse side of a typed memo. The header is mirrored and reads 'Office Memorandum • UNITED STATES GOVERNMENT'. The page is heavily marked with multiple red and blue stamps, including 'PBB', '16 Ph. 58', and '12-46410 S', as well as handwritten pencil annotations. There are two punch holes at the top.

judge.reason: Description B describes specific stamps, annotations, and punch holes as visual content, while Description A describes the page as only having mirrored, illegible text bleed-through and no such visual elements.
